current.summary: Resolved MOAT open issues #6 (scan_status) and #7 (risk_tier); expanded OWASP coverage map to all six relevant standards with links; committed and pushed.

activeWork.description: Resolving open design issues in docs/moat-revised-outline.md
activeWork.progress: Issues #1, #2, #3 already resolved. #6 and #7 resolved this session. #4 (manifest pagination) and #8 (revocation) remain. #5 (consumer motivation) deferred until protocol design settles.

git.lastCommit: a236cd4 docs: resolve open issues #6 and #7, expand OWASP coverage map

recentDecisions: scan_status: REQUIRED field, result: not_scanned valid; structured scanner array {name, version}; no normative staleness; open scanner name list

risk_tier: REQUIRED field; registry-assigned via static analysis (not self-declared); advisory (not normative gating); capability-based L0-L3 rubric + not_analyzed + indeterminate; per-item not transitive; unknown tier strings fail to not_analyzed for forward-compat
OWASP coverage expanded to 6 lists: CI/CD Top 10, Agentic Applications Top 10 (ASI prefix), Web App Top 10:2025, LLM Top 10:2025, Agentic Skills Top 10 (AST prefix), API Security Top 10:2023
Noted: CICD-SEC-8 (federation trust), API2, API7, API10 are gaps not yet specified in the protocol
nextSteps: Work through issue #8: revocation/denouncement mechanism — manifest section vs separate feed URL, client behavior on already-installed denounced content, cross-registry propagation
Work through issue #4: registry manifest size and pagination — now that #6 and #7 are resolved we know what a manifest entry looks like
After #4 and #8 resolved, write issue #5 (consumer motivation / spec introduction) — explicitly deferred until protocol design is settled

context.notes: The original claimed handoff was in the syllago repo (.handoffs/moat-open-questions-resolution.json). This handoff continues the same work stream but is written to the moat repo. The reviewer persona (from panel/agent-ecosystem-feedback-gist.md) was used to stress-test #6/#7 decisions — their main concerns were alert fatigue on not_scanned and probabilistic risk_tier for skills vs hooks. Both acknowledged in the resolved issue text. The 6-7-tradeoffs.md file in panel/ was also used as input and contains S1-S12 stress scenarios worth reviewing if revisiting these decisions.

previousSessions.summary: Deep design session on content type distribution and provenance for syllago and MOAT. Resolved skills frontmatter architecture, created canonical SKILL.md frontmatter spec, updated MOAT outline with OWASP AST10 findings.
completedWork: Skills frontmatter stays agentskills.io-compatible base + adds permissions + requires
Provenance belongs at registry level (MOAT), not individual files
MOAT open issues #6, #7, #8 added to outline
